title: Chaetogastra campanularis
intro: Chaetogastra campanularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the bellflower tree or bell-flower bush. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Chaetogastra campanularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and also used in tinctures and decoctions to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Chaetogastra campanularis belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a species of plant in the genus Chaetogastra. The genus Chaetogastra is classified under the subfamily Vernonioideae. This classification is part of the larger order Asterales. Chaetogastra campanularis is also known to be native to South America.
